channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: How Insurance Leaders Can Leverage AI Without Sacrificing Trust
item.description: In 2025 alone, data breaches affected nearly 300 million individuals, underscoring how quickly trust can erode. Advances in AI, especially Gen AI and Agentic AI, are creating significant opportunities but also sharpening a critical question for insurers: just because a process can be automated, should it be? High-impact AI use cases offer the potential to reduce costs and risk; improve productivity and efficiency; and drive revenue across sales, underwriting, claims and servicing.

item.title: Insurer Nationwide Operating Income Up 37% in 2025
item.description: Insurer Nationwide finished 2025 with net operating income up 37% compared with 2024, to $4.3 billion as total sales and premiums were up 7% to a record $73.2 billion. The Columbus, Ohio-based mutual insurance company—entering its 100th year of operation in 2026—released its 2025 operating results late last week. During a year marked by wildfires and severe convective storms, Nationwide said it paid more than $20.2 billion in claims and benefits to policyholders in 2025.

item.title: Australia’s new merger control regime makes ‘positive start’, regulator says
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FSB Regional Consultative Group for Asia meets in Hanoi
item.description: The Financial Stability Board (FSB) Regional Consultative Group for Asia (RCG Asia) convened on 8-9 April 2026 in Hanoi, hosted by the State Bank of Vietnam. Co-chaired by Michele Bullock, Governor of the Reserve Bank of Australia, and Pham Thanh Ha, Deputy Governor of the State Bank of Vietnam, the meeting covered: global and regional financial vulnerabilities, the impact of strains in foreign exchange markets, safety and security of cross-border payments, and vulnerabilities arising from global stablecoin arrangements. Members also reflected on the FSB’s 2026 work programme and discussed how regional perspectives can contribute to global financial stability efforts.

item.title: Global Investors Help Boost Latin America’s Late-Stage Funding Boom In Q1
item.description: A boom in late-stage and growth funding helped buoy venture funding in Latin America for the first quarter of 2026, Crunchbase data shows. Startups in Latin America raised a combined $1.03 billion across seed- and growth-stage deals in the three-month period ending March 31. Historically, Brazil has been the powerhouse in Latin America for venture capital funding.

item.title: Florida Peninsula raises Palm Re 2026-1 cat bond target again, to $250m
item.description: Now, sources have told us that the target size has been raised further, with Florida Peninsula now seeking $250 million in indemnity based Florida named storm reinsurance from its latest cat bond, while the tranche of notes on offer have been lowered once again. With Florida Peninsula raising its target once again for its latest catastrophe bond, as well as lowering the price of the notes on offer once more, it clearly shows that the insurer is looking to capitalise on catastrophe bond investor appetite to potentially gain more reinsurance support from the capital markets at more attractive pricing. Florida Peninsula Insurance Company has for a second time lifted the targeted amount of reinsurance it is seeking from its latest catastrophe bond, with the Palm Re Ltd.

item.title: PERILS raises Windstorm Goretti insured market loss estimate to €479m
item.description: Catastrophe data aggregator PERILS has published its second industry loss estimate for extratropical windstorm Goretti, also known as Elli, that affected southwestern England, northern France and Belgium in early January, placing the insurance market loss at EUR 479 million. PERILS has confirmed that an updated estimate of the insurance market loss from Windstorm Goretti will be published on July 9th, 2026, six months after the event end date. As per PERILS, windstorm Goretti also marked the second-largest European windstorm event loss in the 2025/26 season, behind windstorm Nils, which struck one month later.

item.title: Cat bonds – Maintaining an equilibrium in value, for sponsors & investors: ILS NYC 2026 video
item.description: The next video we are publishing is from a panel session focused on the catastrophe bond market segment, titled “Cat bonds: Maintaining an equilibrium in value, for both sponsors and investors.” This cat bond focused panel discussion was kindly moderated by Philipp Kusche , Chairman HCMA Europe, Global Co-Head ILS, Howden Capital Markets & Advisory. Watch the full video from our Artemis ILS NYC 2026 conference to more about conditions in the catastrophe bond marketplace, from the perspective of both investors and sponsors . This new video features another panel discussion from Artemis ILS NYC 2026, a session focused on the cat bond marketplace , from our tenth catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) conference in the ILS NYC series, held on February 6th 2026 in New York.

item.title: Mapfre Re appoints Fernando Utrilla Agüero as CFO
item.description: The Board of Directors of Mapfre Re, the global reinsurance unit of Mapfre Group, has approved the appointment of Fernando Utrilla Agüero as Chief Financial Officer (CFO), succeeding Daniel Quermia. Agüero’s appointment is part of the succession process following Quermia’s upcoming retirement, after nearly 40 years with Mapfre Re. Agüero joined Mapfre Re in May 1994 and has since held various positions of responsibility.

item.title: Smaller pension scheme buy-ins rise 30% in 2025: Hymans Robertson
item.description: While 2025 saw a more than 20% fall in bulk annuity volumes year-on-year, the volume of buy-in deals less than £100 million in value increased by over 30%, according to Hymans Robertson’s latest report. Hymans Robertson also noted that 2025 saw developments in the alternative risk transfer space, with TPT announcing plans to launch a superfund. Michael Abramson, Partner and Risk Transfer Specialist at Hymans Robertson, said, “2025 saw a more than 20% fall in bulk annuity volumes year-on-year.

item.title: AXA XL appoints Poonam Jivram as Head of Motor Underwriting, UK & Lloyd’s
item.description: AXA XL, the property, casualty, and specialty risk division of AXA, has announced the appointment of Poonam Jivram as Head of Motor Underwriting, UK & Lloyd’s. Based in London, Jivram will be responsible for developing and executing the strategic underwriting direction for the UK & Lloyd’s motor fleet insurance business, driving profitable growth and strengthening relationships with brokers and clients. Ben Kinsella, Head of Risk Managed Underwriting, UK & Lloyd’s at AXA XL, commented, “One of the major challenges facing the commercial motor fleet market is the growth in electric vehicles and the differing risk profile these vehicles pose.

item.title: China Confirms Three Ships Passed Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Three Chinese ships recently sailed through the Strait of Hormuz after coordination with relevant parties, a foreign ministry spokesperson told a regular daily press briefing on Tuesday, while calling for peace and stability in the Gulf Region. Ship-tracking data showed two Chinese container ships sailed through the Strait of Hormuz on Monday on their second attempt to leave the Gulf after turning back on Friday. “The Strait of Hormuz and the surrounding waters are an important route for global trade and energy supplies.

item.title: Trump Appeals Civil Fraud Ruling, Citing Former Lawyer’s Testimony Doubts
item.description: Donald Trump has asked New York’s highest appeals court to vacate a judge’s finding that he was liable for inflating the value of his real estate assets, arguing that his former lawyer Michael Cohen has since cast doubt on his own testimony against the president. In a filing on Wednesday, Trump’s lawyers cited a January Substack post by Cohen in which he claimed he felt “compelled and coerced” to deliver testimony that New York Attorney General Letitia James was seeking in a civil fraud suit she brought against Trump. In March, Trump’s legal team asked the same appeals court to compel James’ office to produce all its records pertaining to Cohen, arguing his testimony was “at the heart of the NYAG’s case.” In response to a request for comment on Wednesday’s appeal, Cohen said he stood by his Substack post.

item.title: DOJ Top Antitrust Litigators Exit After Ticketmaster Accord
item.description: The Justice Department settled an antitrust case accusing Live Nation of illegally monopolizing the live events industry one week after the start of a New York federal jury trial. The US Justice Department’s top antitrust litigator and three senior trial attorneys that led cases against Live Nation Entertainment Inc., Apple Inc. Some of the departures stem from anger among the antitrust staff about a surprise March 9 settlement with Live Nation and concerns about whether the division is committed to litigating pending cases, according to the people, who asked not to be named discussing internal agency dynamics.

item.title: BOXX Insurance expands distribution as cyber solutions go live on mShift Marketplace
item.description: The post BOXX Insurance expands distribution as cyber solutions go live on mShift Marketplace appeared first on Insurtech Insights . BOXX Insurance has made its cyber insurance solutions available on the Millennial Shift Technologies (mShift) Marketplace, in a move aimed at expanding digital distribution and improving broker access to cyber cover. “By bringing BOXX Insurance onto the mShift Marketplace, we’re giving brokers faster access to innovative cyber insurance and protection solutions while simplifying the placement process through a fully digital experience.” Jim Miller added: “BOXX’s partnership with mShift benefits our broker partners as it expands the accessibility of our all-in-one cyber insurance offerings for SMEs.

item.title: $53m profit: VIG Re posts 17.8% earnings growth on strong underwriting and investment performance
item.description: The post $53m profit: VIG Re posts 17.8% earnings growth on strong underwriting and investment performance appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Strong profitability, a robust solvency position, and a resilient balance sheet enable us to fund growth priorities under VIGRe28, deploy capital selectively, and sustain long-term value creation in a volatile risk environment.” The results underline continued profitability momentum among reinsurers, as disciplined underwriting and lower catastrophe losses support earnings, while investment income remains a key contributor in a higher-rate environment. VIG Re has reported a 17.8% year-on-year increase in profit before tax to €49m (approximately $53m) for FY’25, supported by disciplined underwriting, effective risk management, and solid investment performance.

item.title: Atrium deploys AI-powered ‘BrokerBrief’ with Sofix to accelerate claims handling at Lloyd’s
item.description: The post Atrium deploys AI-powered ‘BrokerBrief’ with Sofix to accelerate claims handling at Lloyd’s appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Atrium and Sofix have deployed an AI-powered claims tool, BrokerBrief, aimed at accelerating claims handling and improving decision-making across all classes of business. The rollout marks a further step in Atrium’s technology strategy to enhance claims handling, while reinforcing Sofix’s focus on bringing AI-driven automation to complex claims workflows across the London market.

item.title: INSURICA raises $25 million
item.description: INSURICA has raised $24.8 million in an exempt securities offering, according to a recent Form D filing. INSURICA, established in 1959, is among the 50 largest insurance brokers in the United States and ranks as the 30th largest privately held independent agency. The full amount of $24,766,101 has already been sold to a single investor, with the offering structured as an equity raise under Rule 506(c).

item.title: Chewy acquires Modern Animal to push deeper into pet healthcare
item.description: Chewy is buying Modern Animal , a tech-enabled veterinary platform with 29 clinics and a membership-driven care model, as it looks to expand beyond commerce and into full-stack pet healthcare. Modern Animal contributes more than $125 million in annualized revenue and expands Chewy’s clinic footprint from 18 to 47 locations nationwide. Modern Animal operates a hybrid model that blends physical clinics with 24/7 virtual care, supported by its own software stack.

item.title: Intact takes majority stake in Cartan Trade
item.description: Cartan Trade is deepening its relationship with Intact Insurance, which is set to become the majority shareholder as part of its European expansion strategy. Effective May 1, Cartan Trade will serve as the underwriting platform for Intact’s trade credit insurance business across Europe, combining its underwriting and technology capabilities with Intact’s balance sheet and AA- rating. The structure gives Intact a dedicated platform for trade credit in Europe, while positioning Cartan Trade to scale through additional capital and distribution.

item.title: Ciberseguro: de la póliza reactiva al modelo de resiliencia operativa
item.description: El mercado actual exige una nueva generación de soluciones que transformen el impacto del ciberriesgo en una variable financiera controlada, sin olvidar que la póliza es solo una parte de la compleja ecuación de resiliencia que las empresas deben resolver hoy mismo. En Stoïk, defendemos un modelo donde la prevención no es un evento puntual, sino un proceso continuo. Esta integración operativa garantiza una transparencia total y elimina los costes ocultos derivados de intermediarios externos en momentos donde cada segundo cuenta.

item.title: Alex Perdel, nuevo Chief Growth & Transformation Officer en Guai Insurtech
item.description: Guai Insurtech ha incorporado a Alex Perdel como Chief Growth & Transformation Officer (CGTO), cargo desde el que asumirá la dirección de la estrategia de crecimiento, tecnología e inteligencia artificial de la filial tecnológica de Grupo Cuidacasa. Perdel supervisará también la evolución del ERP propietario GUAI , integrando nuevas automatizaciones y flujos operativos, y coordinará una arquitectura cloud híbrida sobre AWS y Azure que dará soporte a todas las aplicaciones y servicios de inteligencia artificial. Desde este puesto, liderará la automatización de procesos, el desarrollo de plataformas internas y la evolución del ERP corporativo GUAI, columna vertebral digital de la compañía que conecta procesos, datos y herramientas de todas las áreas de negocio.

item.title: Arch impulsa su negocio ciber con dos promociones internas para Europa e Iberia
item.description: Arch Insurance ha promocionado a José Carlos Jiménez Fernández y a Rafael Ortiz Losada a responsables de Riesgos Cibernéticos, el primero para Europa, y el segundo para Iberia, ambos con base en Madrid. Anteriormente responsable de Riesgo Cibernéticos para Iberia, Jiménez Fernández se incorporó a Arch en 2023 y antes de esto fue Cyber Manager para el Sur de Europa, Oriente Medio, y América Latina en Tokio Marine HCC . Los nombramientos, con efecto inmediato, responden al objetivo de impulsar el crecimiento del negocio cyber en el continente y reforzar la estrategia de suscripción y desarrollo de producto.

item.title: Sedgwick: Australian Product Recalls Declined in Second Half of 2025
item.description: Sedgwick’s 2026 State of the Nation Australian Product Safety and Recall Index shows the five sectors the company monitors in Australia experienced more events in the second half of 2025 compared with the the first half of 2025. Pharmaceutical products and the food and drinks sectors saw big increases in product recalls in Australia in the second half of 2025, a new report shows. The most recalls in the second half of 2025 were issued for passenger cars with 189,873.

item.title: General Motors Recalling 270,000 Cars in US Over Rearview Camera Issue
item.description: General Motors is recalling 271,770 cars in the U.S. over an issue with the rearview camera, the U.S. Related: GM Moves to Expand Its Dealers’ Used-Car Business Related: Ford Recalling 420,000 Vehicles in US Over Windshield Wiper Failure (Reporting by Shah in Bengaluru; Editing by Rashmi Aich) Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: FAA Wants to Fine American Airlines for Alleged Drug, Alcohol Violations
item.description: Federal Aviation Administration said on Wednesday it was proposing a $255,000 civil penalty against American Airlines for allegedly violating drug and alcohol regulations. The FAA said between May 2019 and December 2023, the airline allowed 12 flight attendants who had tested positive on drug and alcohol tests to resume performing safety-sensitive duties without completing all the required follow-up testing. The company has 30 days after receiving the FAA’s enforcement letter to respond to the agency, the administration said in a statement.

item.title: China’s P&C underwriting profit to improve as capital stays strong despite growing asset risk: Moody’s
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: VIG Re sees mature Asia GWP slip in 2025 despite strong overall results
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Coface names Darren Chan country manager for Malaysia, head of Philippines
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Asta announces six senior leadership promotions
item.description: Asta, a Davies-owned third-party managing agency at Lloyd’s, has announced several senior leadership promotions, including the promotion of David Hopkins to Chief Underwriting Officer (CUO), having served as the company’s Deputy CUO since August 2024. He has also held leadership roles at Argo Group, Axis Capital, and Antares Managing Agency. Asta has promoted Clare Barley to Managing Director, where she will provide greater direct support to the CEO in managing Asta’s daily operations.

item.title: Liberty completes APAC licence consolidation and brand simpliﬁcation
item.description: Liberty International Insurance, part of Liberty Mutual Insurance Group, has announced the completion of its Asia Paciﬁc licence consolidation and the simpliﬁcation of its brand to “Liberty” across key markets in the region. The completion of the Hong Kong licence consolidation uniﬁes the Liberty Insurance and Liberty Specialty Markets operations under a single insurance licence in Hong Kong. While the business will trade under the unified “Liberty” brand in Singapore, Hong Kong, and Australia, it will retain the existing brand names in Malaysia, India, and China.

item.title: Duck Creek aims to simplify programme management & enhance financial control for insurers
item.description: Duck Creek Technologies, a provider of software solutions for property and casualty (P&C) and general insurance, has launched Duck Creek Reinsurance with Active Delivery, a new solution that aims to help insurers simplify programme management and improve financial control. “We are excited to introduce Duck Creek Reinsurance with Active Delivery, which brings a new level of functionality and control to reinsurance operations,” said Celine Thierry, Senior Director of Product Management, Reinsurance at Duck Creek Technologies. The firm explains that this “groundbreaking solution that eliminates the need for costly upgrades and empowers insurers to manage complex reinsurance programs at scale in an era of rapid market change and risk diversification.” Built on the AWS platform, Duck Creek Reinsurance with Active Delivery provides insurers with monthly product updates automatically.

item.title: Hormuz Stays Blocked for Now as Hundreds of Ships Seek Exit
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remained largely blocked on Wednesday, as shipowners try to understand if they can safely transit the vital waterway following a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran that was announced overnight. Just three ships were observed leaving the region on Wednesday, according to ship-tracking data compiled by Bloomberg. Some had links to Iran, and the country’s media subsequently reported that passage for tankers will remain blocked after attacks on Lebanon.

item.title: Paramount President Jeff Shell to Depart After Lawsuit Scandal
item.description: Jeff Shell is stepping down as president of Paramount Skydance Corp. Paramount said the board conducted “a complete and thorough review” of the allegations that Shell violated securities disclosure rules and said the facts demonstrated that they “do not establish a securities law violation.” Paramount said Shell has “elected to transition from his positions” as president and a member of the board to focus on this lawsuit. But Shell became the subject of a scandal in recent weeks after R.J.

item.title: Israel Intensifies Lebanon Strikes With Ceasefire in Doubt
item.description: Israel launched its largest assault on Lebanon since the start of its invasion, escalating the campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah even after the US announced a ceasefire in the wider Middle East conflict. The barrage came after Trump said Washington and Iran had agreed to a two-week ceasefire, though it wasn’t clear if Israel’s campaign in Lebanon was part of the agreement. Iran could withdraw from the deal announced by US President Donald Trump if Israel’s Lebanon campaign continues, according to the semi-official Tasnim news agency.

item.title: Brasil aprobará nuevas reglas para el uso de datos de clientes en el open finance
item.description: El Banco Central de Brasil (BC) está preocupado por el intercambio de datos que se hace vía open finance fuera del entorno regulado. Por ejemplo, una empresa minorista que usa esos datos para evaluar el score de crédito de un consumidor, y una oficina contable que accede, con autorización del cliente, a la información bancaria de una empresa para optimizar su trabajo. Por ello, propuso una serie de cambios a la actual regulación que serán sometidos a la aprobación de la junta directiva del regulador, antes de publicarse entre abril y mayo.

item.title: Strait back to business? Ceasefire positive signal, but brokers expect gradual process for Hormuz rates, traffic
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Nantucket Flight Returns After Part of Cabin Door Opens Midair
item.description: A Cape Air flight departing from the Massachusetts island of Nantucket returned safely after part of the main cabin door opened shortly after takeoff, an alarming midair incident that prompted the crew to turn back. The flight had departed from Nantucket Memorial Airport on Monday when the upper portion of the main cabin door opened, the airline said. A passenger video shared on Instagram appears to show the upper portion of the cabin door ajar during the flight, with open sky and ocean visible through the gap.

item.title: The Baldwin Group launches Azimuth Re
item.description: The Baldwin Group announced the launch of Azimuth Re, Ltd., a new member-owned group captive developed in partnership with Innovative Captive Strategies and tailored for Baldwin’s construction clients. By creating a peer group of best-in-class contractors and equipping them with industry-leading risk management, claims, and safety advisory, we’re building a model designed to outperform the standard insurance market while empowering our clients to transform risk management discipline into a competitive advantage.” – Andy O’Brien, Partner at The Baldwin Group. It is going to be a place for our like-minded top performers to outpace the market while being on the vanguard of creativity in structure and return.” – Greg Deems, Senior Partner, The Baldwin Group.

item.title: Smart appoints Lee Hepplewhite as CTO
item.description: Smart Pension has appointed Lee Hepplewhite as Chief Technology Officer, as the company looks to scale its product and engineering capabilities. In his new role, Hepplewhite will focus on strengthening engineering capabilities, shaping technology strategy, and advancing the product roadmap as Smart expands in the UK and internationally. Hepplewhite brings over 20 years of experience across AI, machine learning, cloud modernization, and data platforms.

item.title: Westland names EVP of client experience and digital
item.description: Westland Insurance has appointed Cari Watson as Executive Vice President, Client Experience and Digital, effective March 30, 2026. Watson brings more than 25 years of industry experience and has been with Westland since 2020, where she has led efforts to align strategy with execution and improve client experience through technology and organizational design. Watson joins the executive team to lead digital transformation and brand strategy, with a focus on enhancing client engagement and supporting organic growth.

item.title: Kingstone Selects ZestyAI Model for California Expansion
item.description: “ZestyAI’s Z-FIRE model provides the property-level intelligence we need to underwrite with precision in a complex market such as California.” Kingstone says the model enables more granular risk differentiation by analyzing property-specific characteristics, including building materials, vegetation, topography and defensible space. (Kingston, N.Y.) has selected the Z-FIRE wildfire risk model from ZestyAI (San Francisco, Calif.) to support its planned entry into the California homeowners insurance market. The model will be used to assess wildfire exposure at the individual property level, supporting rating and underwriting decisions as Kingstone expands on an excess and surplus lines basis.

item.title: Genpact Integrates Parallel API to Enhance AI Research Workflows
item.description: (Image source: Parallel Web Systems homepage.) Genpact (New York) has integrated web research capabilities from Parallel Web Systems (Palo Alto, Calif.) into its enterprise AI architecture to automate workflows and improve decision-making across insurance and sales operations. “While automation attempts struggle with real-world complexity, especially in highly regulated industries, our partnership with Parallel replaces repetitive human effort with continuous agentic research that integrates seamlessly into enhanced decision-making systems,” says Sanjeev Vohra, Chief Technology and Innovation Officer, Genpact. The integration embeds Parallel’s API into Genpact’s systems, enabling real-time data retrieval, automated research and structured outputs to support business processes, including property/casualty claims.

item.title: AI Escalates Fraud Risk and Detection Arms Race
item.description: AI is enhancing insurers’ ability to detect fraud—but it is also equipping fraudsters with more powerful tools, accelerating an ongoing arms race. While detection and prevention efforts have improved over time, fraud remains widespread, costly, and often underreported. Artificial intelligence is rapidly becoming integral to insurance operations, including underwriting, claims processing, and fraud detection.

item.title: Toilet Paper Warehouse in California Destroyed by Fire; Employee Arrested
item.description: has been arrested on arson charges after a massive fire broke out Tuesday at the facility in California that serves around 50 million people. The 1.2 million-square-foot warehouse — located in Ontario, about 35 miles outside of Los Angeles — houses facial tissue and toilet paper, according to a local Fox report. Ontario Deputy Fire Chief Mike Wedell told Bloomberg News the building’s roof completely collapsed and all products inside were destroyed.

item.title: Telenor Sued Over Claims It Exposed Myanmar Customers to Junta Repression
item.description: A Swedish non-profit filed a class action lawsuit against Telenor on Tuesday on behalf of over 1,200 people whose phone data it says the Norwegian telecom’s Myanmar subsidiary shared with the country’s military junta following a 2021 coup. Telenor, which has since exited Myanmar, said it believed there was nothing in the lawsuit that the company had not already addressed and “in our view it is unlikely that such a claim will succeed.” The lawsuit, filed in Norway by the Justice and Accountability Initiative, alleges Telenor Myanmar shared the call logs and location data of suspected political opponents of the junta, exposing them to repression and leading to the execution of at least one prominent activist and the prosecution of another. ‘Terrible’ If Data Was Misused By Junta, Telenor Says Telenor, which is 54% owned by the Norwegian state, was informed of plans to file the suit in a pre-action letter in October and said at the time it had been “legally required to provide traffic data to the authorities.” “Telenor Myanmar was operating on the ground in a war zone,” the company said in an email to Reuters on Wednesday, adding that refusing the military authorities’ requests could have led to “imprisonment, torture or the death penalty” for local employees.

item.title: Alert Fatigue Drags Down IT Production Environments, Leads to Costly Outages
item.description: A new survey on IT production environments sheds light on the growing problem of alert fatigue. Based on a survey of 1,039 SRE, DevOps and IT operations professionals, the 2026 State of Production Reliability and AI Adoption Report reveals “an industry at an inflection point: reactive, alert-driven incident response is no longer sufficient for the scale and complexity of modern production environments, and the path forward requires autonomous systems that can prevent, resolve and optimize operations end to end.” “This data highlights a gap in how today’s tools support modern production environments,” said Gou Rao, CEO and co-founder of NeuBird AI. Current production environments outpace incident management practices built to support them, and this deficiency is now leading to measurable failures, the survey showed.

item.title: Berkshire-owned PacifiCorp Utility Ruling Win Could Reduce Oregon Wildfire Damages
item.description: An Oregon appeals court on Wednesday ruled in favor of PacifiCorp in litigation over a series of Oregon wildfires in 2020, saying a trial judge erred in letting the case against the Berkshire Hathaway-owned utility proceed as a class action. The decision by the Oregon Court of Appeals in Salem could reduce PacifiCorp’s overall liability, which the utility has estimated could total tens of billions of dollars. PacifiCorp has denied claims by thousands of Oregon residents and business owners who accused it of causing several wildfires by negligently failing to shut off power lines during a Labor Day weekend windstorm in 2020.

item.title: U.S. E&S Growth Slows Again; Declining Berkshire Volume Tops Leaders
item.description: Across the industry, S&P GMI attributed the overall slowdown in E&S premium growth to declining commercial property premiums amid intensified market competition. A recent analysis by S&P Global Market Intelligence reveals that the pace of growth in the U.S. excess and surplus lines market slowed to 7.8% in 2025, marking the first single-digit growth rate recorded since 2017.

item.title: Franklin Templeton: No evidence of weakening fundamentals in cat bonds, stays overweight
item.description: Franklin Templeton Investment Solutions, the hedge fund-focused arm of the global asset manager, has maintained its overweight conviction on the catastrophe bond asset class, with the firm citing that while yields and spreads have compressed compared to last year, the market remains healthy, with no evidence of weakening fundamentals. “Although yields and spreads have compressed compared to this time last year, the market remains healthy, with no evidence of weakening fundamentals.” The firm noted that by mid-March, spreads had widened from year-end levels, which was primarily driven by seasonal technical factors rather than underlying fundamentals. However, on the renewal side, approximately US$14.7 billion of catastrophe bonds are scheduled to mature in 2026, compared to US$13.7 billion in 2025, which as Franklin Templeton notes, implies roughly US$1 billion of incremental new issuance potential compared to last year’s figure.
